mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above two percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in retail sales and housing starts demonstrates broad-based demand strength that outperforms either indicator alone in predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and HOUST does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond individual series in explaining 20-day SPX returns

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment during late-cycle expansions signals imminent earnings downgrades and recession risk, producing sharply negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are non-negative in greater than 60% of late-cycle instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.2 percentage points

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates improving manufacturing utilization and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following monthly INDPRO increases show Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding three percent forces the Federal Reserve to maintain a hawkish stance, raising real discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.0%

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in housing starts and payroll employment signals broad demand momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal of HOUST and PAYEMS fails to achieve Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a negative supply-demand shock that causes severe negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of walk-forward observations
